pipeline.input_prompt: Build an event booking platform with venues, tickets, payments, QR check-in, and organizer analytics.

stage_status.summary: Normalized permissions, deduped surfaces, and completed business rules.
Simulated routes, UI, API, DB, auth, permissions, and business rules.

app.name: Event Management Builder
app.description: Build an event booking platform with venues, tickets, payments, QR check-in, and organizer analytics.

intent.primary_goal: Build an event booking platform with venues, tickets, payments, QR check-in, and organizer analytics.

entities.name: customer
entities.description: Customer records used by the event_management app.

entities.name: booking
entities.description: Booking records used by the event_management app.

entities.name: resource
entities.description: Resource records used by the event_management app.

entities.name: venue
entities.description: Venue records used by the event_management app.

entities.name: ticket
entities.description: Ticket records used by the event_management app.

entities.name: payment
entities.description: Payment records used by the event_management app.

entities.name: check_in
entities.description: Check In records used by the event_management app.

entities.name: subscription
entities.description: Subscription records used by the event_management app.

entities.name: analytics_event
entities.description: Analytics Event records used by the event_management app.

design.data_model_summary: 9 domain entities compiled into relational tables with inferred relationships.
design.business_rules_summary: Role access, ownership checks, CRUD permissions, and audit-safe persistence.
design.summary: Build an event booking platform with venues, tickets, payments, QR check-in, and organizer analytics.

business_logic.name: role-access
business_logic.description: Protected pages and APIs require declared roles and permissions.

business_logic.name: ownership-restriction
business_logic.description: Users may update only records they own unless they have manage permission.

business_logic.name: admin-analytics-access
business_logic.description: Admin analytics requires admin role and read:analytics permission.

steps: rendered page Login with auth layout
rendered page Dashboard with dashboard layout
rendered page Customers with crud layout
rendered page Bookings with crud layout
rendered page Resources with crud layout
rendered page Venues with crud layout
rendered page Tickets with crud layout
rendered page Payments with crud layout
rendered page Check Ins with crud layout
rendered page Subscriptions with crud layout
rendered page Admin Analytics with analytics layout
created table customers(created_at, description, id, name, owner_id)
created table bookings(created_at, description, id, name, owner_id)
created table resources(created_at, description, id, name, owner_id)
created table venues(created_at, description, id, name, owner_id)
created table tickets(created_at, description, id, name, owner_id)
created table payments(amount, created_at, id, status, subscription_id)
created table check_ins(created_at, description, id, name, owner_id)
created table subscriptions(id, plan, renewal_at, status, user_id)
created table analytics_events(actor_id, created_at, event_name, id)
created table users(email, id, password_hash, role)
